close_reason: Added ?Sized bound to all impl blocks, enabling CachingStore<dyn SecretStore>
description:
src/lib.rs:46-50: pub struct CachingStore<S: SecretStore> { inner: Arc<S>, ... }. The impl blocks at lib.rs:52, 67, 116 all use S: SecretStore (and S: WritableSecretStore for the writable impl). The implicit Sized bound means you cannot construct CachingStore<dyn SecretStore>.

This conflicts with secretx-core's documented public API contract. From secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1235: 'callers store backends as Arc<dyn SecretStore>'. A caller that holds Arc<dyn SecretStore> (e.g. a config-driven backend selector) cannot wrap it in a CachingStore without first downcasting to a concrete type.

Concrete consequence: any code path that uses dynamic dispatch for backend selection — which is the documented intended pattern — is forced to either (a) move caching out of CachingStore and reimplement it, or (b) convert to monomorphic dispatch by enumerating concrete backends.

Fix: relax the bounds to S: SecretStore + ?Sized in the struct definition and impl blocks. The fields (Arc<S> and the unrelated Mutex) are already Sized regardless of S. Verify with:
    let c: CachingStore<dyn SecretStore> = CachingStore::new(arc_dyn, ttl);

Test coverage: add an integration test in tests/caching_store.rs that constructs CachingStore<dyn SecretStore> from an Arc<dyn SecretStore> to lock this in.

Alternative (rejected): introduce a separate DynCachingStore type. This duplicates the implementation and forces callers to choose at the type level when they shouldn't have to.

Note: also update the writable impl. Arc<dyn WritableSecretStore> wrapping is the same use case.
title: CachingStore<S> cannot wrap Arc<dyn SecretStore> due to S: Sized bound
